function.name: table_query
function.description: Query the FULL raw data of local .csv/.xlsx files the user registered as local access folders. Use this when the user asks for specific values, totals, averages, counts, min/max, filtering, or sorting over their local spreadsheet/CSV data (e.g. "3월 매출 합계", "가장 비싼 항목", "row count"). This reads the original file directly with no row limit, so prefer it over rag_search for numeric or per-row questions about local tables. Do not use it for prose documents, web research, or general knowledge questions.

operation.description: list_tables: list available local table files with their columns. describe: show one table's columns, inferred types, row count and sample rows. query: run a filter/aggregate query and return matching rows or aggregated values.

file_name.description: Target table file name, e.g. 'sales_2025.xlsx' or 'metrics.csv'. Required for describe and query. Use list_tables first when unsure which files exist.

sheet_name.description: Worksheet name for .xlsx files. Defaults to the first sheet.

columns.description: Columns to return for a row query. Default: all columns.

where.description: Row filter conditions, combined with AND. Numeric comparison is used when both sides are numbers; otherwise string comparison.

group_by.description: Columns to group by before aggregation.

aggregate.description: Aggregations computed over matching rows (per group when group_by is set). Use column '*' with func 'count' to count rows.

sort_by.description: Column (or aggregate label like 'sum(매출)') to sort result rows by.

limit.description: Maximum number of result rows to return (1-200).
